Title: 4 Reasons Altria Is A 7.3% Yield Retirees Can Trust 
 Content: ['This article was coproduced with Dividend Sensei. ', "Do you dream of a secure and prosperous retirement? Or do you think that's impossible?", 'It isnâ\x80\x99t if you use disciplined financial science combined with blue-chip dividend stocks.', 'Consider the example of Anne Scheiber, who had no formal financial training on Wall Street. An IRS employee, she started with just $5,000 yet amassed a $22 million fortune over 50 years.', 'According to ETFguide.com, her highest salary in any given year was just $3,100. But she invested what she had into stocks little by little.', ' ', 'â\x80\x9câ\x80¦ over time, her portfolio grew from just a few companies to over 140 stocks, including giants like Bristol-Myers Squibb, Coca-Cola, Chrysler, and Paramount...', 'â\x80\x9cAlthough Scheiber was a multimillionaire on paper, even generating more than $200,000 a year in dividend income at one point, she still lived in a nondescript apartment with peeling paint from the walls. She was the epitome of frugality."', 'In all, Mrs. Scheiber achieved 18.3% compound annual growth rate (CAGR) annual returns for 50 years â\x80\x93 just 2.5% less than Warren Buffett.', '', '(Source)', 'She did this via a tax-efficient, blue-chip, buy-and-hold approach after carefully studying stock market history and following the principles of disciplined financial science.', 'This brings us to today and the four reasons why Dividend Sensei â\x80\x93 and now my newly monied son â\x80\x93 are buying shares in Altria (NYSE:MO). Altria has managed to grow about 9% over decades, turning $1 into $140 in inflation-adjusted, dividend-gushing wealth in just the last 34 years.', 'And over the past 15, disciplined income investors have been able to generate as much as 25% annual returns.', 'Today, Altria is in a bear market though â\x80\x93 giving disciplined income investors the chance to earn rich retirement-making future returns. Hereâ\x80\x99s whyâ\x80¦', '', 'In recent days, Altria has plunged as much as 10% due to some scary headlines. Its $12.8 billion, 35% stake in Juul Labs (JUUL) is under fire, for one thing. A judge just tentatively ruled against it concerning a proposed antitrust class action suit.', 'And undoing that business deal would be complicated, to say the least.', 'The Biden administration, meanwhile, is considering the idea of mandating that tobacco companies lower cigarettesâ\x80\x99 nicotine levels. Drastically.', 'So much so that these products could no longer be legally deemed addictive. And Seeking Alpha adds that â\x80\x9ca menthol ban under consideration would target smoking in younger people, many of whom start with menthol, according to The Wall Street Journal.â\x80\x9d', 'It sounds like Altriaâ\x80\x99s worst nightmare. Yet Morningstar says thereâ\x80\x99s â\x80\x9clittle evidenceâ\x80\x9d to think the government will actually go through with its reported musings.', ' ', 'â\x80\x9câ\x80¦ so we maintain our valuations and wide moat and negative trend ratings for the cigarette makers with exposure to the U.S. cigarette marketâ\x80¦', 'â\x80\x9cNegative headlines such as this have spooked investors since 2017, and the tobacco group is undervalued relative to our estimate of intrinsic value."', "If enacted, yes, the menthol ban could take a significant toll on Altria's medium-term growth rates. But thatâ\x80\x99s a decade-old idea at this point. It's not a new risk and has been priced into Altria's historical fair value for years.", 'And hereâ\x80\x99s another point to consider: There would no doubt be a long legal fight to criminalize menthol in the U.S. British American Tobacco (BTI) â\x80\x93 which incidentally survived Europeâ\x80\x99s ban in 2018 â\x80\x93 actually thinks it would take 5-7 years to sort out if it happened at all.', 'As for regulating nicotine, Bank of America (BAC) wrote up a report on that conceptâ\x80¦ that still ended by maintaining its Buy rating on Altria.', 'U.S. cigarette volumes have been falling every single year for over half a century now. Yet MO has delivered 18% annual returns over that time, becoming the second best-performing U.S. stock behind Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B).', '', '(Source: earnings presentation)', 'Management expects about 5% declines in volume from here, with the key word being â\x80\x9cexpects.â\x80\x9d That means it can take appropriate action beforehand.', 'So far, cost cutting and price hikes have allowed MO to keep growing its cigarette profits at a steady rate decade after decade.', 'Remember those amazing 18% annual returns itâ\x80\x99s delivered over that time? Thatâ\x80\x99s grown $1 into $3,927 ($566, adjusted for inflation).', "Thirty-nine percent of those returns came from dividends being reinvested during Altria's frequent bear markets - bear markets that were always caused by scary headlines, usually about proposed regulations.", 'In other words, those claiming that Altria is on death\'s door are betting that "this time is finally different." And maybe it\'s in terms of those scary headlines coming true. But 80% of the time, blue chips like Altria can adapt and overcome challenges.', 'As it is, the companyâ\x80\x99s long-term growth guidance is for 5%-7%, which it plans to achieve like this:', '', '(Source: earnings presentation)', 'In other words, MO has a plan to eventually sell no cigarettes whatsoever. Itâ\x80\x99s doing the very opposite of betting the farm on its biggest product.', 'Globally, nicotine is a $450 billion market thatâ\x80\x99s growing at 4% annually. But Altria understands that, in 50 years or so, cigarettes will probably be gone altogether.', 'As such, its future is in reduced-risk nicotine, cannabis, and alcohol products.', '', "Altria's investment thesis is built around steadily rising cash flow and earnings. And it also does the sensible thing of buying back its stock during its admittedly frequent bear markets.", '', "In just the next three years, MO is expected to generate $6.2 billion in post-dividend retained earnings. That's enough to pay off 21% of its debt or buy back 2%-2.5% of its stock every year.", "For the record, those earnings already include investments in reduced-risk products, or RRPs. And management's policy is to pay out 80% of earnings as dividends.", 'The remainder is used to repurchase stock or strengthen its balance sheet.', "Analysts expect MO's buyback potential to be about 2%-3% per year. So even if earnings growth stopped entirely, it could drive about 2.5% earnings per share, free cash flow and dividend growth just by repurchasing its shares.", 'That would more than offset long-term inflation expectations.', '', "Minus any abject catastrophes where the sky falls, MO's balance sheet is solid and expected to steadily improve over time. Which brings us back to the Juul investment.", 'At the time, Altriaâ\x80\x99s 35% stake was a reasonable move to make. It involved paying 45x earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) for a company growing more than 100% annually.', 'In hindsight, we can say the timing was very poor, given it was right before the vapocalypse and pandemic. In fact, Altria itself has admitted the deal was its single biggest capital allocation error ever.', 'And itâ\x80\x99s written almost all of Juul as a result. Thatâ\x80\x99s why the CEO at the time resigned.', "With all that said, this partial acquisition wasnâ\x80\x99t expected to pay dividends to Altria for many years â\x80\x93 if ever. The debt MO took on didn't leverage its balance sheet to dangerous levels.", 'Therefore, its dividend was never in peril.', '', "In 2020, the S&P 500 saw 14% EPS declines. Yet Altria's revenue went up 3% despite incredible supply chain and disruption.", 'In fact, the last time it had negative earnings growth was the 2% it experience in 2003.', "Weâ\x80\x99re hardly looking at a growth stock here, to be sure. But the stable, steady progress it's making has its dividend looking ever more attractive.", 'Itâ\x80\x99s already a dividend king. So itâ\x80\x99s hard to see it changing its attractive payout policy from here.', "As weâ\x80\x99ve already hinted, Altria's risk profile has been relatively stable for 50-plus years.", 'Outside of bear markets and bubbles, hundreds of millions of investors for 20 years have paid 14x-16x earnings for Altria. So thereâ\x80\x99s a 91% probability that its intrinsic value falls in this range.', 'Yet, today, MO trades at 10.4x forward earnings. According to the Graham/Dodd fair-value formula, that accounts for just 1% CAGR over time. Altria is currently trading at a 25% discount to fair value based on 2021 consensus fundamentals and the conservative end of historical fair value market multiples of its fundamentals and current dividend.', 'Source: Dividend Kings', 'For anyone comfortable with its risk profile, MO is a potentially very strong buy today.', '', "(Source: Yahoo Finance, Reuters'/Refinitiv) 14 out of 18 analysts", 'So the consensus growth range is 4.4%-6% CAGR. Management expects 5%-7%. Morningstar estimates 4% to 6%. And Fitch estimates 7%.', "It doesn't take a math PhD to see that those numbers are much higher than the 1% itâ\x80\x99s priced for.", 'Altria is expected to grow at a much slower rate than in the past, itâ\x80\x99s true. But its higher yield and more modest growth are still likely to deliver exceptional 13% long-term returns.', 'Hereâ\x80\x99s a reasonable idea of what kind of returns you can expect buying Altria todayâ\x80¦', 'If it grows as analysts expect through 2023 â\x80\x93 which it historically always does â\x80\x93 and returns to its short historical fair value, then analysts expect 77% total returns and 23.6% CAGR returns.', 'The S&P 500, meanwhile, should hit -1.7% CAGR.', "Using the low end of MO's historical fair value â\x80\x93 which would be justified by 4%-7% growth â\x80\x93 it can deliver Buffett-like returns over the next three years. In fact, at its approximate 25% discount, it has the potential to outperform the 35% overvalued S&P 500 by 81% in that time.", 'If MO grows as analysts expect through 2026 and returns to historical fair value, you could expect 126% total returns and 15.4% CAGR.', 'Thatâ\x80\x99s vs. 4.6% CAGR for the S&P 500.', 'And over the very long term, analysts expect 7.3% yield + 5.6% growth = 12.9% CAGR (after valuation changes cancel out). Or you could see it as a range of 11.3%-14.3%.', 'Management, for its part, expects 12.3%-14.3%. Whereas the S&P is looking at 7.9% and dividend aristocrats are around 10.6%.', 'If you take nothing else from this article, consider this: Nicotine is like alcohol. People have used it for millennia and will likely always do so.', "Thus, it's reasonable to assume that MO might be able to grow at 4%-7% annually for the next 50 years. It's overcome incredible secular industry and regulatory headwinds throughout its history to become the second best-performing stock in U.S. history. And weâ\x80\x99re looking forward to seeing where it goes from here.", 'There are no risk-free companies. And no company is right for everyone.', 'Morningstar sees Altriaâ\x80\x99s risks as being lower than they were a decade or two ago. But newer regulatory risks are real and not to be ignored. For instance, â\x80\x9cThe recent ban on flavored nicotine liquids demonstrates the FDA is willing to take significant steps to prevent the uptake of nicotine products by new consumers.â\x80\x9d ', 'As already noted, it considers a complete menthol prohibition â\x80\x9cunlikely.â\x80\x9d And hereâ\x80\x99s a bit more on regulatory risks:', ' ', 'â\x80\x9cThe potential for limits on the nicotine levels in cigarettes is a relatively new risk, however, with the FDA announcing in the summer of 2017 that it will investigate the potential for nicotine control. We do not believe such controls are a foregone conclusion however, because they could have unforeseen consequences such as increasing cigarette volumes.', 'â\x80\x9cThe introduction of plain packaging would be detrimental to Altria\'s wide economic moat and remains a low probability risk."', "Overall though, Altria's risk profile is little changed over the last 50 years â\x80\x93 including when it comes to headline risk. So, essentially, as long it successfully adapts and overcomes any actual regulatory changes?", 'Today could be a wonderful opportunity to buy more.', "In total, Dividend Kings uses six rating agencies and 18 analysts to monitor Altria's risk profile. These 24 experts collectively know MO's business inside and out.", 'Management, meanwhile, says investors can expect about 13% CAGR long-term total returns in the future. With the track record of success that Altria has, weâ\x80\x99re more than willing to give it the benefit of the doubt.', 'Like Anne Scheiber, we know that correctly pricing risk is the key to getting rich on Wall Street. And the risk just doesnâ\x80\x99t seem very big to us based on everything we see.', 'Today, Altria is priced for 1% growth instead of the 4%-7% analysts expect. Their forecasts are historically among the most accurate of any company on Wall Street.', 'Yes, a lot still could go wrong still. (They always can for any stock.) And scary headlines will continue to cause MO to periodically fall off a cliff.', 'But itâ\x80\x99s proven to be a great investment at the past when trading at a very safe 7.3% yield and 10.4x earnings. And we donâ\x80\x99t expect this time around to be any different in the end.', "Note: Brad Thomas is a Wall Street writer, which means he's not always right with his predictions or recommendations.
